title: workflow
text: vignette, apply multi-scale spatial analysis workflow shown Wayfarer paper, different dataset. Xenium dataset paper mechanotransducer Piezo1 coordinates metabolism inflammation promote skin growth, investigates glycolysis inflammation mouse skin expansion, mediated Piezo1. Skin expansion performed injecting saline subdermal implant. relevant skin expansion occurs pregnancy obesity, performed create skin skin grafting. dataset two samples (believe biological replica) per condition: Non-expanded – implants inserted expanded Expanded, 14 days post inflation (PI14) Expanded control (PI7) Expanded skin topical Yoda1 treatment (PI7) activates Piezo1 vignette, perform multi-scale spatial analysis Wayfarer identify genes gene co-expression whose multi-scale spatial patterns differ across conditions.
code: library(Wayfarer) library(Voyager) library(SpatialFeatureExperiment) library(sf) library(BiocParallel) library(alabaster.sfe) library(dplyr) library(readr) library(ggplot2) library(stringr) library(purrr) library(tidyr) library(scater) library(R.utils) theme_set(theme_bw())

title: workflow
text: First, apply spatial aggregation various bin sizes one sample perform basic analyses. can analyses visualizations samples. tissue boundary computed concave hull cell centroids images provided authors. See SpatialFeatureExperiment::getTissueBoundaryConcave(). purpose demonstration, can start sample expanded1, one expanded skin samples 14 days post injection. example dataset uploaded OSF can downloaded functions Wayfarer package.  Also download transcript spots transcript spots, can create spatial aggregates range bin sizes (microns) tissue boundary used remove bins entirely outside tissue. Often transcript spots detected outside tissue. Also unlike Wayfarer paper, hexagonal bins rather square ones used show hexagonal bins also work. Since takes run, purpose rendering vignette GitHub Actions, results uploaded OSF can downloaded Decompress current directory Bins overlap little tissue outliers analyses cause artifacts, bins overlap tissue proportion removed. proportions found manual tuning LUAD data used Wayfarer paper. Percentiles can also used quantiles argument. value supplied bin size. runBinAnalyses performs basic analyses bin size removing bins overlap little tissue: Log normalize data, using proportion bin overlapping tissue size factor instead total counts. Using proportion vs. total counts makes big difference downstream analyses. Non-spatial PCA log normalized data Moran’s gene, log normalized data Lee’s L gene pairs, log normalized data Since analyses can take run, results can also downloaded OSF
code: tb <- Piezo1TissueBoundary(sample = "expanded1") plot(st_geometry(tb)) (tx_path <- Piezo1TxSpots("expanded1")) #>                                                          expanded1  #> "/home/runner/.cache/R/BiocFileCache/e0ccda281f8_expanded1.csv.gz" (sides <- sort(c(2^(3:8), 12 * 2^(0:4)))) #>  [1]   8  12  16  24  32  48  64  96 128 192 256 binned_path <- makeAggregates(tx_path,                               out_path = "expanded1", sample_id = "expanded1",                               tech = "Xenium", tissue_boundary = tb, sides = sides,                               flip_geometry = FALSE,                               square = FALSE, BPPARAM = MulticoreParam(2, progressbar = TRUE)) (binned_path <- Piezo1Binned("expanded1")) #>                                                           expanded1  #> "/home/runner/.cache/R/BiocFileCache/e0cc2108640f_expanded1.tar.gz" untar(binned_path) min_props <- c(0.9, 0.9, 0.85, 0.85, 0.8, 0.7, 0.6, 0.5, 0.4, 0.3, 0.3) names(min_props) <- sides runBinAnalyses("expanded1", "expanded1/bin_analyses", tissue_geometry = tb,                min_props = min_props,                BPPARAM = MulticoreParam(2, progressbar = TRUE)) (bin_analyses_path <- Piezo1BinAnalyses("expanded1")) #>                                                                        expanded1  #> "/home/runner/.cache/R/BiocFileCache/e0cc1598bc79_expanded1_bin_analyses.tar.gz" untar(bin_analyses_path)

title: workflow
text: Load one bin sizes take look, 16 micron bin diameter   circle PC1 PC2 looks interesting  Rotate tissue save space plotting
code: (sfe <- readObject("expanded1/bin_analyses/bin16_esda/")) #> >>> Reading SpatialExperiment #> >>> Reading colgeometries #> >>> Reading spatial graphs #> Warning in sn2listw(df, style = style, zero.policy = zero.policy, #> from_mat2listw = TRUE): neighbour object has 5 sub-graphs #> Warning in mat2listw(as(altReadObject(dddd), "CsparseMatrix"), style = #> method$args$style, : neighbour object has 5 sub-graphs #> class: SpatialFeatureExperiment  #> dim: 100 9748  #> metadata(0): #> assays(2): counts logcounts #> rownames(100): Acaca Adgre1 ... Gsdmc Ngf #> rowData names(2): moran_expanded1 K_expanded1 #> colnames(9748): 956 1113 ... 78406 78407 #> colData names(6): sample_id overlap_props ... total sizeFactor #> reducedDimNames(1): PCA #> mainExpName: NULL #> altExpNames(0): #> spatialCoords names(2) : X Y #> imgData names(4): sample_id image_id data scaleFactor #>  #> unit: micron #> Geometries: #> colGeometries: bins (POLYGON)  #>  #> Graphs: #> expanded1: col: poly2nb ElbowPlot(sfe) plotReducedDim(sfe, "PCA", ncomponents = 4) + geom_density2d() plotDimLoadings(sfe) sfe <- rotateMinRect(sfe) plotSpatialFeature(sfe, "Col17a1") spatialReducedDim(sfe, "PCA", ncomponents = 4, ncol = 1, divergent = TRUE, diverge_center = 0)

title: workflow
text: Next see Moran’s changes bin sizes sample  gene curve. see different kinds curves: genes higher Moran’s small scales decrease larger scales. form peak 16 32 microns bin diameter. form peak 64 128 microns. can cluster curves better visualize different patterns: Hierarchical clustering Leiden used cluster curves, original values differences adjacent bin sizes (hclust_diffs leiden_diffs). Plot clusters  Plot cluster medians  Plot Moran’s curves mean variance Moran’s null hypothesis spatial autocorrelation, 2.5% 97.5% quantiles Bonferroni correction number genes bin sizes
code: df_moran <- read_csv("expanded1/bin_analyses/df_moran.csv") #> Rows: 1100 Columns: 4 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: "," #> chr (2): gene, sample #> dbl (2): moran, side #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. plotMoranCurves(df_moran) df_moran2 <- clusterMoranCurves(df_moran) names(df_moran2) #> [1] "moran"        "gene"         "side"         "sample"       "hclust"       #> [6] "leiden"       "hclust_diffs" "leiden_diffs" plotMoranCurves(df_moran2, facet_by = "leiden") plotClusterMedians(df_moran2, "leiden") # Need to read all bin sizes for the spatial neighborhood graphs sfes <- readBins("expanded1/bin_analyses", sides = sides) #> >>> Reading SpatialExperiment #> >>> Reading colgeometries #> >>> Reading spatial graphs #> >>> Reading SpatialExperiment #> >>> Reading colgeometries #> >>> Reading spatial graphs #> >>> Reading SpatialExperiment #> >>> Reading colgeometries #> >>> Reading spatial graphs #> Warning in sn2listw(df, style = style, zero.policy = zero.policy, #> from_mat2listw = TRUE): neighbour object has 5 sub-graphs #> Warning in mat2listw(as(altReadObject(dddd), "CsparseMatrix"), style = #> method$args$style, : neighbour object has 5 sub-graphs #> >>> Reading SpatialExperiment #> >>> Reading colgeometries #> >>> Reading spatial graphs #> Warning in sn2listw(df, style = style, zero.policy = zero.policy, #> from_mat2listw = TRUE): neighbour object has 2 sub-graphs #> Warning in mat2listw(as(altReadObject(dddd), "CsparseMatrix"), style = #> method$args$style, : neighbour object has 2 sub-graphs #> >>> Reading SpatialExperiment #> >>> Reading colgeometries #> >>> Reading spatial graphs #> >>> Reading SpatialExperiment #> >>> Reading colgeometries #> >>> Reading spatial graphs #> >>> Reading SpatialExperiment #> >>> Reading colgeometries #> >>> Reading spatial graphs #> >>> Reading SpatialExperiment #> >>> Reading colgeometries #> >>> Reading spatial graphs #> >>> Reading SpatialExperiment #> >>> Reading colgeometries #> >>> Reading spatial graphs #> >>> Reading SpatialExperiment #> >>> Reading colgeometries #> >>> Reading spatial graphs #> >>> Reading SpatialExperiment #> >>> Reading colgeometries #> >>> Reading spatial graphs mean_vars <- getMoranMeanVar(sfes, "moran") plotMoranCurves(df_moran2, facet_by = "leiden", mean_vars = mean_vars,                 show_null = TRUE)

title: workflow
text: Also read Lee’s L; make clustering easier, cutoff 0.2 set gene pairs Lee’s L lower 0.2 bin sizes removed. Due large number gene pairs, smaller subset can plotted prevent plot becoming solid block  gene pair curve. gene pairs higher Lee’s L smaller scales decrease larger scales. peak 16 32 microns. peak 64 128 microns. low Lee’s L small scales increase larger scales. negative Lee’s L small scales become positive larger scales. weakly negative Lee’s L smaller scales becomes lower intermediate scales. Cluster Lee’s L curves better visualize patterns   Unfotunately can’t plot interval null hypothesis like Moran’s mean variance Lee’s L null hypothesis spatial autocorrelation differ gene pair, depending Pearson correlation. ’s spatial pattern, gene pairs high Pearson correlation can still get moderate value away 0. addition, computing mean variance requires dense square matrix, can computed larger bin sizes number bins smaller.
code: df_lee <-readLeeSamples("expanded1/bin_analyses", cutoff = 0.2) plotLeeCurves(df_lee, sample_n = 1000) df_lee2 <- clusterLeeCurves(df_lee) plotLeeCurves(df_lee2, facet_by = "hclust_diffs", show_median = TRUE) +     geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 2, color = "gray") plotClusterMedians(df_lee2, "leiden")

title: workflow
text: plot gene expression space different bin sizes Randomly select gene Leiden cluster 4  can also make kind plot Lee’s L, bivariate palette. Randomly select gene pair
code: # Reduce empty space for (i in seq_along(sfes)) {     sfes[[i]] <- rotateMinRect(sfes[[i]]) } set.seed(29) gene_use <- df_moran2 |>      filter(leiden == 4) |>      pull(gene) |>      sample(1) plotSFEs(sfes[c("16", "48", "96", "256")], gene_use, show_sizes = TRUE, ncol = 1) set.seed(29) pair_use <- df_lee2 |>      filter(hclust_diffs == 4) |>      pull(pair) |>      sample(1) |>      str_split(pattern = "_", simplify = TRUE) |>      as.vector() plotSFEsBiscale(sfes[c("16", "48", "96", "256")], pair_use[1], pair_use[2],                  df_lee = df_lee, ncol = 1, legend_index = 1,                 heights = rep(1, 5))

title: workflow
text: seeing Moran’s Lee’s L curves one sample, one may say, “Interesting, ?” ’ll compare curves across multiple biological conditions see genes gene pairs differ multi-scale manner may apparent one spatial scale analyzed. get sample biological condition Run whole pipeline samples; takes run (1 half hours laptop 4 cores), can download results OSF render vignette fast.
code: data("sample_info") sample_info #> # A tibble: 8 × 2 #>   sample        condition    #>   <chr>         <chr>        #> 1 expanded1     PI14         #> 2 expanded2     PI14         #> 3 expanded1_pi7 PI7          #> 4 expanded2_pi7 PI7          #> 5 nonexpanded1  non-expanded #> 6 nonexpanded2  non-expanded #> 7 yoda1         Yoda1        #> 8 yoda2         Yoda1 tbs <- Piezo1TissueBoundary("all") tx_paths <- Piezo1TxSpots("all") for (s in sample_info$sample[-1]) {     tb <- tbs$geometry[tbs$sample == s]     makeAggregates(tx_paths[s],                out_path = s, sample_id = s,                tech = "Xenium", tissue_boundary = tb, sides = sides,                flip_geometry = FALSE,                square = FALSE, BPPARAM = MulticoreParam(2, progressbar = TRUE))     runBinAnalyses(s, file.path(s, "bin_analyses"), tissue_geometry = tb,                min_props = min_props,                BPPARAM = MulticoreParam(2, progressbar = TRUE)) } # Download from OSF bin_analyses_dirs <- Piezo1BinAnalyses(sample = "all") for (s in bin_analyses_dirs[-1]) untar(s)

title: workflow
text: use linear mixed models (LMM) spline term model Moran’s curve, random effect intercept slope biological condition intercept slope. non-expanded samples many small pieces cells, Moran’s Lee’s L curves rather erratic. ’re excluded following analyses. proportion genes significant random effects? proportion significant random slopes? plot top 9 genes significant random effects, meaning either intercept slope differ among different conditions.  Also plot genes significant random slopes, .e. slopes spline terms differ among conditions  difference seems small genes; LMM take account different variances Moran’s different bin sizes. Plot one genes space different samples
code: dirs_use <- file.path(sample_info$sample, "bin_analyses") df_morans <- readMoranSamples(dirs_use, sample_info = sample_info) df_morans_lmm <- df_morans |>      filter(condition != "non-expanded") |>      dplyr::rename(value = moran, group = condition, feature = gene) |>      runBinLMM(BPPARAM = SerialParam()) #> Warning: There were 25 warnings in `mutate()`. #> The first warning was: #> ℹ In argument: `pvals = bplapply(...)`. #> Caused by warning: #> ! Model failed to converge with 1 negative eigenvalue: -2.4e-03 #> ℹ Run `dplyr::last_dplyr_warnings()` to see the 24 remaining warnings. mean(df_morans_lmm$p_random_adj < 0.05) #> [1] 0.6 mean(df_morans_lmm$p_slope_adj < 0.05) #> [1] 0.28 genes_use <- df_morans_lmm |>      filter(p_random_adj < 0.05) |>      slice_max(log_p_random_adj, n = 9) |>      pull(feature) data("ditto_colors") df_morans |>      filter(gene %in% genes_use, condition != "non-expanded") |>      ggplot(aes(side, moran, group = sample)) +     geom_line(aes(color = condition)) +     scale_color_manual(values = ditto_colors) +     scale_x_continuous(transform = "log2") +     facet_wrap(~ gene) +     labs(x = sprintf("Bin size (\u03BCm)"), y = "Moran's I") genes_use <- df_morans_lmm |>      filter(p_slope_adj < 0.05) |>      slice_max(log_p_slope_adj, n = 9) |>      pull(feature) df_morans |>      filter(gene %in% genes_use, condition != "non-expanded") |>      ggplot(aes(side, moran, group = sample)) +     geom_line(aes(color = condition)) +     scale_color_manual(values = ditto_colors) +     scale_x_continuous(transform = "log2") +     facet_wrap(~ gene) +     labs(x = sprintf("Bin size (\u03BCm)"), y = "Moran's I") sfes <- readBinSamples(file.path(sample_info$sample, "bin_analyses"),                        side = 32) # Reduce empty space for (i in seq_along(sfes)) {     sfes[[i]] <- rotateMinRect(sfes[[i]]) } plotSFEs(sfes, "Lgr6", ncol = 2, widths = c(1,1), show_sizes = FALSE) &     theme(legend.position = "none")

title: workflow
text: Also run LMM Lee’s L curves, see multi-scale behaviors Lee’s L differ among conditions. cutoff 0.2 , gene pairs absolute values Lee’s L 0.2 bin sizes samples removed. takes minutes run proportion gene pairs significant random effects? proportion significant random slopes? Plot gene pairs significant random effects  differences pronounced Moran’s . can due differences PI7 (Yoda1 also PI7) PI14, sometimes seems Yoda1 treatment makes gene co-expression PI7 like PI14. Also plot gene pairs significant random slopes  Plot one gene pairs space
code: df_lees <- readLeeSamples(dirs_use, sample_info = sample_info, cutoff = 0.2) df_lees_lmm <- df_lees |>      filter(condition != "non-expanded") |>      dplyr::rename(value = lee, group = condition, feature = pair) |>      runBinLMM(BPPARAM = SerialParam()) #> Warning: There were 1064 warnings in `mutate()`. #> The first warning was: #> ℹ In argument: `pvals = bplapply(...)`. #> Caused by warning: #> ! Model failed to converge with 1 negative eigenvalue: -7.0e-02 #> ℹ Run `dplyr::last_dplyr_warnings()` to see the 1063 remaining warnings. mean(df_lees_lmm$p_random_adj < 0.05) #> [1] 0.6265244 mean(df_lees_lmm$p_slope_adj < 0.05) #> [1] 0.245122 pairs_use <- df_lees_lmm |>      slice_max(log_p_random_adj, n = 9) |>      pull(feature) df_lees |>      filter(condition != "non-expanded") |>      dplyr::rename(group = condition) |>      plotLeeSelect(df_lees_lmm, pairs_use) pairs_use <- df_lees_lmm |>      slice_max(log_p_slope_adj, n = 9) |>      pull(feature) df_lees |>      filter(condition != "non-expanded") |>      dplyr::rename(group = condition) |>      plotLeeSelect(df_lees_lmm, pairs_use) sfes <- readBinSamples(file.path(sample_info$sample, "bin_analyses"),                        side = 128) # get rid of that one annoying bin from a smaller piece of tissue sfes[[6]] <- findDebrisCells(sfes[[6]], distance_cutoff = 300) sfes[[6]] <- sfes[[6]][,!sfes[[6]]$is_debris] # Reduce empty space for (i in seq_along(sfes)) {     sfes[[i]] <- rotateMinRect(sfes[[i]]) } plotSFEsBiscale(sfes, "Ldhb", "Pfkm", df_lees, ncol = 2, widths = c(1,1),                  show_sizes = FALSE, side_use = 64, legend_index = 6)

title: workflow
text: find gene pairs significant Lee’s L random effects random slopes known interactions CellChat’s database, without actually using CellChat package (CRAN Bioconductor). Plot gene pairs CellChat’s database  Plot one space
code: df_lee_pathways <- getCellChatInfo(df_lees_lmm, genes = rownames(sfes[[1]]),                                    species = "mouse") df_lee_pathways #> # A tibble: 9 × 39 #>   pair            p_slope p_random p_main p_slope_adj  p_random_adj p_main_adj #>   <chr>             <dbl>    <dbl>  <dbl>       <dbl>         <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 Itgb1_Tnc    0.00000210 3.87e-10 0.0465   0.0000888 0.00000000509     0.0963 #> 2 Itgb1_Tnc    0.00000210 3.87e-10 0.0465   0.0000888 0.00000000509     0.0963 #> 3 Cdh1_Itgb1   0.00409    8.45e- 3 0.0637   0.0233    0.0156            0.113  #> 4 Col4a1_Itgb1 0.350      5.17e- 5 0.0345   0.588     0.000167          0.0842 #> 5 Col4a1_Itgb1 0.350      5.17e- 5 0.0345   0.588     0.000167          0.0842 #> 6 Col4a1_Itgb1 0.350      5.17e- 5 0.0345   0.588     0.000167          0.0842 #> 7 Col4a1_Itgb1 0.350      5.17e- 5 0.0345   0.588     0.000167          0.0842 #> 8 Col4a1_Itgb1 0.350      5.17e- 5 0.0345   0.588     0.000167          0.0842 #> 9 Col4a1_Itgb1 0.350      5.17e- 5 0.0345   0.588     0.000167          0.0842 #> # ℹ 32 more variables: log_p_slope_adj <dbl>, log_p_random_adj <dbl>, #> #   log_p_main_adj <dbl>, interaction <int>, interaction_name <chr>, #> #   pathway_name <chr>, ligand <chr>, receptor <chr>, agonist <chr>, #> #   antagonist <chr>, co_A_receptor <chr>, co_I_receptor <chr>, #> #   annotation <chr>, interaction_name_2 <chr>, evidence <chr>, #> #   is_neurotransmitter <chr>, ligand.symbol <chr>, ligand.family <chr>, #> #   ligand.location <chr>, ligand.keyword <chr>, ligand.secreted_type <chr>, … pairs_use <- df_lee_pathways |>      pull(pair) |> unique() df_lees |>      filter(condition != "non-expanded") |>      dplyr::rename(group = condition) |>      plotLeeSelect(df_lees_lmm, pairs_use) plotSFEsBiscale(sfes, "Itgb1", "Tnc", df_lees, ncol = 2, widths = c(1,1),                  show_sizes = FALSE, side_use = 64, legend_index = 6) # Clean up unlink(sample_info$sample, recursive = TRUE)

what: Download Xenium data from Xue et al. — Piezo1-download

code: Piezo1TxSpots(   sample = c("all", "expanded1", "expanded2", "expanded1_pi7", "expanded2_pi7",     "nonexpanded1", "nonexpanded2", "yoda1", "yoda2"),   bfc = BiocFileCache() )  Piezo1TissueBoundary(   sample = c("all", "expanded1", "expanded2", "expanded1_pi7", "expanded2_pi7",     "nonexpanded1", "nonexpanded2", "yoda1", "yoda2"),   bfc = BiocFileCache() )  Piezo1Binned(   sample = c("all", "expanded1", "expanded2", "expanded1_pi7", "expanded2_pi7",     "nonexpanded1", "nonexpanded2", "yoda1", "yoda2"),   bfc = BiocFileCache() )  Piezo1BinAnalyses(   sample = c("all", "expanded1", "expanded2", "expanded1_pi7", "expanded2_pi7",     "nonexpanded1", "nonexpanded2", "yoda1", "yoda2"),   bfc = BiocFileCache() )

what: Aggregate transcript spots into bins of varying sizes — makeAggregates
title: Aggregate transcript spots into bins of varying sizes — makeAggregates
text: function aggregates transcript spots smFISH-based spatial transcriptomics technologies bins various sizes. resulting gene count matrix, count eah gene bin number transcript spots gene intersecting bin. QC original dataset strongly recommended spatial aggregation.

title: Aggregate transcript spots into bins of varying sizes — makeAggregates
code: makeAggregates(   tx_file,   out_path,   sides = sort(c(2^(3:8), 12 * 2^(0:5))),   sample_id = "sample01",   tech = c("other", "Vizgen", "Xenium", "CosMX"),   spatialCoordsNames = c("X", "Y"),   gene_col = "gene",   phred_col = "qv",   min_phred = 20,   tissue_boundary = NULL,   flip_geometry = TRUE,   BPPARAM = SerialParam(),   tx_parquet_path = NULL,   ... )

title: Aggregate transcript spots into bins of varying sizes — makeAggregates
text: tx_file File transcript spot coordinates. Can directory multi-file parquet. out_path Output directory sides Side length (square bins) hexagon diameter microns. specifies size bins used aggregate transcripts. sample_id sample SFE object transcript spots added . tech Technology whose standard output transcript file . "", arguments spatialCoordsNames gene_col ignored column names standard output used instead. spatialCoordsNames Column names x, y, optionally z coordinates spots. defaults Vizgen. gene_col Column name genes. phred_col Column name Phred scores spots. min_phred Minimum Phred score keep spot. default 20, conventional threshold indicating "acceptable", meaning 1 chance spot decoded error. tissue_boundary Optional recommended. sf, sfc, sfg tissue boundary polygon. See getTissueBoundaryImg getTissueBoundaryConcave getting tissue boundary SFE, removing debris QC. tissue boundary used remove bins overlap tissue. QC performed aggregation, remove debris every single aggregation. flip_geometry Logical, whether flip transcript spot geometries match images added later. BPPARAM bpparam object specify parallel computing genes. lot memory used, stick `SerialParam()`. Using multicore R level `save_memory = TRUE` works results faster computation. tx_parquet_path input Parquet file (e.g. CSV file), re-formatted multi-file Parquet improve speed computation. reformatted files can written path specified argument. NULL, multi-file Parquet written temporary directory. ... arguments passed aggregateTx. excludes cellsize save_memory.

title: Aggregate transcript spots into bins of varying sizes — makeAggregates
text: Invisibly output directory; output written disk   alabaster.sfe disk serializations   SpatialFeatureExperiment objects, one object bin size.   SFE object bin size written subdirectory   out_path named "binx" "x" bin size, e.g. "bin12" 12   micron bins.

title: Aggregate transcript spots into bins of varying sizes — makeAggregates
text: original file transcript spot coordinates parquet format, converted multi-file parquet file gene, greatly improve speed aggregation.

text: df Data frame output clusterMoranCurves, data frame column named "gene" genes, "side" bin size side length, "moran" Moran's values, optionally categorical columns indicating cluster membership genes. name Column name df spatial results. color_by data frame column "gene" gene symbols IDs one column color. Alternatively, name column df use coloring. facet_by Name categorical integer column df facet plot, tidyeval. show_null Logical, whether show expected values Moran's null hypothesis (values randomly permuted space) interval within null rejected (p >= 0.05 Bonferroni correction accounting number genes number sides). mean_vars Data frame means variances Moran's null hypothesis getMoranMeanVar, required show_null = TRUE. color_name Name show colors legend. title_name Name metric put plot title.

Use colGeometryNames look names sf data frames associated cells/spots. bbox Named numeric vector specifying bounding box, either zoom smaller area show box . names "xmin", "xmax", "ymin", "ymax" order. bbox used SFE objects sfes. palette Name bivariate palette; see bi_pal complete list built-palettes `biscale` package. exprs_value assay whose data plotted swap_rownames Column name rowData(object) used identify features instead rownames(object) labeling plot elements. found rowData, rownames gene count matrix used. title Title entire multi-panel plot crop Logical, whether crop samples bbox. FALSE, bbox shown box plot indicate location. show_sizes Logical, whether show bin sizes titles individual panels. what: Remove bins that overlap too little with the tissue boundary — removeEdgeBins
title: Remove bins that overlap too little with the tissue boundary — removeEdgeBins
text: help mitigate edge effect analyzing binned data, especially larger bins. Bins lowest percentiles area overlapping tissue removed. However, perfectly remove edge effect downstream analyses; mitigate edge effect.

title: Remove bins that overlap too little with the tissue boundary — removeEdgeBins
code: removeEdgeBins(sfe, overlap_props, min_prop = 0.9, quantile = NULL)

title: Remove bins that overlap too little with the tissue boundary — removeEdgeBins
text: sfe SFE object aggregated bins, generated makeAggregates. overlap_props numeric vector proportion area bin tissue/cells, single string column colData(sfe) proportions. min_prop Minimum proportion bin area tissue; bins lower proportions removed. default 0.9 suitable small bins bin side less 24 microns; smaller number used larger bins, 0.8 32 microns, 0.5 64 microns, 0.2 192 microns . quantile Bins overlapping proportion quantile removed. NULL, supercede min_prop. can check histogram overlap_props deciding min_prop quantile.

title: Remove bins that overlap too little with the tissue boundary — removeEdgeBins
text: SFE object bins overlap little tissue   removed.

what: Run basic analyses on aggregated SFE objects of all bin sizes — runBinAnalyses

text: function runs log normalization, bin-level QC, PCA, adjacency graph, Moran's , Lee's L binned SFE objects directory, given bins overlapping little tissue removed.

title: Run basic analyses on aggregated SFE objects of all bin sizes — runBinAnalyses
code: runBinAnalyses(   dir,   out_path,   tissue_geometry,   min_props = 0.9,   quantiles = NULL,   ncomponents = 30,   queen = FALSE,   zero.policy = TRUE,   p.adjust.method = "BH",   neg_regex = c("^NegPrb", "^Blank", "^BLANK", "^NegControl", "Unassigned"),   BPPARAM = SerialParam(),   ... )

title: Run basic analyses on aggregated SFE objects of all bin sizes — runBinAnalyses
text: dir Directory binned outputs located. Output bin size must directory named "binx", "bin12" 12 micron bins. out_path Directory write output tissue_geometry Either tissue boundary (holes present tissue) cell segmentation polygons. Area overlap bin geometry computed. min_props Minimum proportion bin overlapping tissue; bins overlap enough removed mitigate edge effect. numeric vector length number bin sizes can differ different bin sizes. length 1, value applied bin sizes. Otherwise, must names corresponding bin sizes. quantiles Numeric vector quantiles area bin overlapping tissue, can differ different bin sizes. NULL, supercede min_props. ncomponents Number components compute PCA. queen TRUE, single shared boundary point meets contiguity condition, FALSE, one shared point required; note one shared boundary point necessarily mean shared boundary line zero.policy default attr(listw, "zero.policy") set listw created, attribute set, use global option value; TRUE assign zero lagged value zones without neighbours, FALSE assign NA p.adjust.method Method correct multiple testing, passed p.adjustSP. Methods allowed p.adjust.methods. neg_regex Character vector regex patterns indicating feature negative control. Features matching patterns removed prior analyses. Counts features kept aggregated input data dir. BPPARAM BiocParallelParam object specifying whether computing metric numerous genes shall parallelized. ... arguments passed S4 method (convenience wrappers like calculateMoransI) method used compute metrics specified argument type (general functions like calculateUnivariate). See documentation functions name specified type spdep package method specific arguments. variograms, see .variogram.

title: Run basic analyses on aggregated SFE objects of all bin sizes — runBinAnalyses
text: Invisibly out_path; log normalization, QC, PCA, adjacency   graph, Moran's stored SFE object; SFE object   results written out_path directory names   "binx_esda". data frame Moran's  Lee's L across bin sizes   written out_path CSV files. "esda" means   exploratory spatial data analysis (ESDA) performed.

what: Convert transcript spot files to multi-file Parquet — toMultifileParquet
title: Convert transcript spot files to multi-file Parquet — toMultifileParquet
text: greatly improves speed spatial aggregation.

title: Convert transcript spot files to multi-file Parquet — toMultifileParquet
code: toMultifileParquet(   tx_file,   tx_parquet_path,   tech = c("other", "Vizgen", "Xenium", "CosMX"),   spatialCoordsNames = c("X", "Y", "Z"),   gene_col = "gene",   phred_col = "qv",   min_phred = 20 )

title: Convert transcript spot files to multi-file Parquet — toMultifileParquet
text: tx_file File transcript spot coordinates. Can directory multi-file parquet. tx_parquet_path input Parquet file (e.g. CSV file), re-formatted multi-file Parquet improve speed computation. reformatted files can written path specified argument. NULL, multi-file Parquet written temporary directory. tech Technology whose standard output transcript file . "", arguments spatialCoordsNames gene_col ignored column names standard output used instead. spatialCoordsNames Column names x, y, optionally z coordinates spots. defaults Vizgen. gene_col Column name genes. phred_col Column name Phred scores spots. min_phred Minimum Phred score keep spot. default 20, conventional threshold indicating "acceptable", meaning 1 chance spot decoded error.

title: Convert transcript spot files to multi-file Parquet — toMultifileParquet
text: Nothing R session; reformatted files written   disk, directory specified tx_parquet_path.
